reproduction_definition: Reproduce the two primary quantitative claims from Schulman et al. 2017 (PPO) using CleanRL v1.0.0 + PyTorch 2.1.0 + gymnasium 0.29.1 + MuJoCo 3.1.1 (DeepMind free release): (1) MuJoCo benchmark (Table 1, Section 6.1): PPO with clipped surrogate objective (epsilon=0.2) achieves an average normalized score >= 0.70 across 7 MuJoCo -v4 continuous control environments x 3 seeds (21 runs), with a target of 0.82 from the paper (tolerance of -0.12 granted for -v4 vs -v1 dynamics drift, ~5-15% expected deviation per ENV003). (2) Atari benchmark (Table 2, Section 6.4, BUDGETED): PPO wins a majority of head-to-head comparisons against A2C across a representative 5-game subset (BeamRider, Breakout, Pong, Qbert, SpaceInvaders) trained for 40M game frames (10M timesteps) and 3 seeds per game; full 49-game reproduction is tracked as a stretch goal requiring GPU. Roboschool (Section 6.3) is EXCLUDED per ENV008 (deprecated, qualitative-only). The reproduction is APPROXIMATE: uses -v4 MuJoCo envs instead of deprecated -v1, PyTorch instead of TF1, and incorporates undocumented but necessary implementation tricks (Adam eps=1e-5, max_grad_norm=0.5, orthogonal init, per-minibatch advantage normalization, value-function loss clipping) that are absent from the paper but present in all reference implementations.
smoke_test_plan: Goal: Validate the full pipeline (environment setup, CleanRL script, tensorboard logging) in under 2 minutes. Command: `python /workspace/cleanrl/cleanrl/ppo_continuous_action.py --env-id HalfCheetah-v4 --total-timesteps 50000 --learning-rate 3e-4 --num-envs 1 --num-steps 2048 --num-minibatches 32 --update-epochs 10 --clip-coef 0.2 --gamma 0.99 --gae-lambda 0.95 --seed 1 --track False`. Pass criteria: (a) script completes without error, (b) at least 1 policy update occurs (>=2048 steps collected), (c) episodic_return logged to stdout is a finite number, (d) no NaN in policy loss or value loss, (e) run completes in <120 seconds on CPU. Smoke test for Atari: `python /workspace/cleanrl/cleanrl/ppo_atari.py --env-id BreakoutNoFrameskip-v4 --total-timesteps 50000 --num-envs 8 --num-steps 128 --num-minibatches 4 --update-epochs 3 --clip-coef 0.1 --anneal-lr True --seed 1 --track False`. Pass criteria: same as above.
full_run_plan: PHASE 1 — MuJoCo Benchmark (Table 1 primary claim, FULL): Run PPO Clip (epsilon=0.2) on all 7 MuJoCo -v4 environments for 3 seeds each (21 runs). Command template: `python /workspace/cleanrl/cleanrl/ppo_continuous_action.py --env-id {ENV} --total-timesteps 1000000 --learning-rate 3e-4 --num-envs 1 --num-steps 2048 --num-minibatches 32 --update-epochs 10 --clip-coef 0.2 --gamma 0.99 --gae-lambda 0.95 --ent-coef 0.0 --vf-coef 0.5 --max-grad-norm 0.5 --seed {SEED} --track False --exp-name ppo_mujoco_{ENV}_seed{SEED}`. Environments: [HalfCheetah-v4, Hopper-v4, InvertedDoublePendulum-v4, InvertedPendulum-v4, Reacher-v4, Swimmer-v4, Walker2d-v4]. Seeds: [1, 2, 3]. Note: --num-minibatches 32 gives minibatch size = (1 * 2048) / 32 = 64, matching paper Table 3. Estimated wall time: ~15-20 min per run on modern CPU/GPU, ~5-7 hours total for 21 runs (parallelizable). PHASE 2 — Atari Benchmark (Table 2, BUDGETED subset): Run PPO on 5 representative Atari games x 3 seeds = 15 runs. Games: [BreakoutNoFrameskip-v4, BeamRiderNoFrameskip-v4, PongNoFrameskip-v4, QbertNoFrameskip-v4, SpaceInvadersNoFrameskip-v4]. Command template: `python /workspace/cleanrl/cleanrl/ppo_atari.py --env-id {GAME} --total-timesteps 10000000 --learning-rate 2.5e-4 --num-envs 8 --num-steps 128 --num-minibatches 4 --update-epochs 3 --clip-coef 0.1 --anneal-lr True --clip-vloss True --ent-coef 0.01 --vf-coef 1.0 --max-grad-norm 0.5 --gamma 0.99 --gae-lambda 0.95 --seed {SEED} --track False --exp-name ppo_atari_{GAME}_seed{SEED}`. Note: --num-minibatches 4 gives minibatch size = (8 * 128) / 4 = 256, matching paper Table 5. --anneal-lr True anneals both LR and clip-coef linearly to 0. Estimated wall time: ~2-4 hours per run on GPU (slow on CPU). STRETCH GOAL — Full 49-game Atari: extend Phase 2 to all 49 Atari games; requires GPU; ~145 GPU-hours total (49 games * 3 seeds * ~1 GPU-hour/run).
dataset_plan: MuJoCo (no data download required): Environments are simulators provided by gymnasium[mujoco] + mujoco==3.1.1 (DeepMind free release). Install via `pip install gymnasium[mujoco]==0.29.1 mujoco==3.1.1`. Set MUJOCO_GL=egl for headless Docker rendering. Use -v4 suffix environments (e.g., HalfCheetah-v4) as -v1 environments were removed in gymnasium>=0.26. IMPORTANT: -v4 envs have slightly different reward scaling vs paper's -v1 envs (accepted deviation, see ENV003). Atari ROMs (pre-installed in Docker): ROMs are installed via `AutoROM[accept-rom-license]==0.6.1` with `AutoROM --accept-license --quiet` during image build (see Dockerfile). Verify with `python -c "import ale_py; import gymnasium; env = gymnasium.make('BreakoutNoFrameskip-v4'); env.reset(); print('OK')"`. Use NoFrameskip-v4 variants (raw ALE without frame-skip wrapper; CleanRL ppo_atari.py applies its own frame-skip and grayscale wrappers). Shimmy >=1.3.0 provides the ale-py <-> gymnasium bridge. No external dataset files, model checkpoints, or pre-collected trajectories are required for either experiment.
evaluation_plan: MUJOCO EVALUATION (matching Section 6.1 and Table 1): Step 1 — Collect returns: For each of 21 runs (7 envs x 3 seeds), record all episodic returns logged during training. Step 2 — Compute mean final performance: For each run, compute mean episodic return over the last 100 episodes (or last 100 logged episodes if fewer are available). Step 3 — Normalize per-environment: For each environment, let random_score = mean return of a random policy (published values: HalfCheetah~-280, Hopper~20, InvertedDoublePendulum~9, InvertedPendulum~0, Reacher~-100, Swimmer~0, Walker2d~1.4; use CleanRL benchmark values if paper values unavailable) and best_score = maximum mean final performance across all 3 seeds for PPO Clip. Normalized score = (run_score - random_score) / (best_score - random_score). Step 4 — Average: Compute grand average over all 21 normalized scores. Target: >= 0.70 (paper reports 0.82 on -v1; -0.12 tolerance for -v4 dynamics drift). Also record per-environment mean ± std across 3 seeds. ATARI EVALUATION (matching Section 6.4 and Table 2): Step 1 — For each game x seed, record the full episode return history during training. Step 2 — Metric A (all-training): Compute mean episode return averaged over the entire training run (all logged episodes). Average across 3 seeds. Step 3 — Metric B (last-100): Compute mean episode return over the last 100 logged episodes. Average across 3 seeds. Step 4 — For each game, compare PPO vs A2C baseline (use CleanRL A2C benchmarks or SB3 A2C benchmarks as the A2C reference since we are not re-running A2C). Declare winner for each game. Step 5 — Count games won. Target for 5-game subset: PPO should win >= 3/5 games on metric A and be competitive on metric B. NORMALIZATION NOTE (A007): Since we only run PPO (not all 11 variants from paper), we use per-environment best score from CleanRL benchmarks as the normalization ceiling rather than paper Table 1 baselines.
verification_checklist: MuJoCo architecture: 2-layer MLP, 64 units each, tanh activations, separate policy and value networks (no shared params), Gaussian policy head, per CleanRL ppo_continuous_action.py
MuJoCo hyperparameters match paper Table 3: LR=3e-4, T=2048, minibatch=64, K=10 epochs, gamma=0.99, lambda=0.95, epsilon=0.2, no entropy bonus (ent-coef=0.0)
Atari architecture: CNN (Conv32/8x8/s4 -> Conv64/4x4/s2 -> Conv64/3x3/s1 -> FC512) with shared policy and value heads, per A3C (Mnih 2016) as assumed in A001
Atari hyperparameters match paper Table 5: LR=2.5e-4 annealed, N=8 actors, T=128, minibatch=256, K=3 epochs, epsilon=0.1 annealed, ent-coef=0.01, vf-coef=1.0
Adam epsilon=1e-5 confirmed (not PyTorch default 1e-8) per ENV004 — check optimizer constructor in cleanrl source
max_grad_norm=0.5 gradient clipping applied before optimizer step per ENV009
Orthogonal weight initialization applied: hidden scale=sqrt(2), policy head scale=0.01, value head scale=1.0 per ENV010
Per-minibatch advantage normalization confirmed: advantages normalized to mean=0, std=1 within each minibatch, not globally
Value-function loss clipping confirmed: clipped VF loss used alongside unclipped in max() formulation
GAE computation uses stopped gradient for V(s_T) bootstrap term per A008
Total timesteps per MuJoCo run = exactly 1,000,000 (paper Section 6.1)
Total timesteps per Atari run = exactly 10,000,000 (= 40M game frames / 4 frame-skip) per Section 6.4
Random seeds used: 3 independent seeds per environment (e.g., 1, 2, 3) with torch.manual_seed and numpy seed set
MuJoCo normalization: (score - random_score) / (best_score - random_score), averaged over all 21 runs
Atari games-won comparison: two separate metrics (all-training avg and last-100-episodes avg) reported per Table 2
MuJoCo normalized score >= 0.70 (primary pass/fail criterion, paper target 0.82 with -0.12 tolerance for -v4)
Atari PPO wins majority of head-to-head game comparisons vs A2C baseline on metric A (all-training avg)
No NaN or infinite values in final metrics.json
Reward curves show monotonically improving trend for at least 4/7 MuJoCo environments
Roboschool section confirmed EXCLUDED with rationale documented (ENV008: deprecated, qualitative-only)
All 21 MuJoCo runs completed (7 envs x 3 seeds) with no crashes or timeouts
Docker container built successfully from provided Dockerfile with all pip installs verified
MUJOCO_GL=egl confirmed set in environment before running any MuJoCo experiment
Atari ROM installation verified: `python -c 'import ale_py; env = gymnasium.make("BreakoutNoFrameskip-v4")'` succeeds without error
